title: quercus breviradiata
intro: Quercus breviradiata, also known as the short-bracted oak, is a species of tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. The medicinal properties of Quercus breviradiata lie in its leaves, which contain tannins and flavonoids, providing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus breviradiata include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Quercus breviradiata is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties.
